_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to analyze tools/hardware used to extract/analyze/capture memory and disk images
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, including file system structure, process management, user and permission models, package management, and common command-line utilities for text processing and system monitoring.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.372), AI-K-013 (AI, cosine: 0.364).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.5/10 (raw 0.247). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, analyze, capture, disk, extract, hardware] vs WIDAI-only terms [command, common, data, engineering, file, fundamentals] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.840 (P=0.834, R=0.845). Balanced P/R — the 11-word DCWF element and 32-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Ability to analyze tools/hardware used to extract/analyze/capture memory and dis" and "Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, i" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.380 > STS 0.247 (gap +0.134). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.3%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, analyze, capture, disk, extract, hardware]. WIDAI terms: [command, common, data, engineering, file, fundamentals].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.134 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
